message:
chore(beads): mark D3 flat-combining work in progress

Refresh the Beads export after the new D3 flat-combining infrastructure landed.

This export updates bd-3wop3.3 from open to in_progress, assigns it, and
records the implementation note that the CommitSequenceCombiner module now
exists with its initial test coverage. The note also captures the remaining
follow-up explicitly: wiring the combiner into TxnManager.alloc_commit_seq() for
production use.

No code changes are included here; this is the planning/status sync that keeps
the issue graph aligned with the repository state.

files.patch:
@@ -1230,7 +1230,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3wop3","title":"EPIC: Eliminate 8-16 Thread Contention — Flip 0.53x to 3x+","description":"## Problem

FrankenSQLite shows 3.7-3.9x improvement at 2-4 threads but DEGRADES to 0.53-0.67x at 8-16 threads due to internal lock contention. Zero corruption, just throughput collapse.

## Root Causes (profiled, not guessed)

7 contention points identified by code analysis:
1. WAL Append Gate: Global Mutex<()> per DB — serializes ALL WAL frame writes
2. PageCache Mutex: Single Mutex<PageCache> — all cache ops go through one lock
3. SSI Registry Handles: O(N) lock acquisition per commit — each commit locks ALL N active handles
4. Condvar notify_all: Thundering herd on every page lock release
5. CommitSequencer AtomicU64: Cache-line ping-pong at ~50ns per fetch_add × 8000 commits
6. CommitIndex LeftRight: Shard contention at 8+ threads
7. SerializedWriteMutex: Indirect drain_concurrent_writers spin

## Strategy (from alien graveyard analysis)

Phase 1 (removes serial bottlenecks):
- Parallel WAL with per-thread log buffers + epoch group commit (§15.4 Silo/Aether)
- Flat Combining for commit sequencer + WAL flush (§14.2)
- Epoch-Based Reclamation for version GC (§14.10)

Phase 2 (multiplier — make reads lock-free):
- Sharded PageCache (PostgreSQL BufMappingPartitionLock pattern)
- Seqlocks for hot metadata reads (§14.9)
- Replace SSI O(N) handle iteration with Bloom filter approximation

Phase 3 (deep wins):
- Replace Condvar notify_all with per-waiter futex
- NUMA-aware cohort locks for multi-socket scaling

## Target
- 8t: 0.67x → 2.0-2.5x
- 16t: 0.53x → 1.5-2.0x","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-20T18:31:06.968409099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T18:31:06.968409099Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contention","performance","scalability","track-d"]}
 {"id":"bd-3wop3.1","title":"D1: Replace global WAL Append Gate with per-thread log buffers + epoch group commit","description":"## The #1 Bottleneck

The WAL Append Gate (pager.rs:29-43) is a global Mutex<()> per database path. EVERY concurrent writer must serialize through it to write WAL frames. At 8+ threads, this single lock serializes all I/O — destroying parallelism.

Current code:
  type WalAppendGate = Arc<Mutex<()>>;
  // Acquired at pager.rs:3131-3135 during WAL frame writing

## Solution: Parallel WAL (Silo/Aether Pattern, Graveyard §15.4)

Replace the single WAL with per-thread log buffers that flush independently.

### Architecture

1. Each writer thread owns a ThreadLocalLogBuffer (Vec<WalFrame>, ~64KB initial)
2. WAL frames are appended to the thread-local buffer with NO global lock
3. A background epoch ticker advances the global epoch every ~10ms
4. On epoch advance, each thread flushes its buffer to its own WAL segment file
5. Commit durability: txn waits until min(all_threads.flushed_epoch) >= txn.epoch
6. Checkpoint merges all segment files into the main DB (existing checkpoint logic)

### Key Design Decisions

- Thread-local buffers: SmallVec<[WalFrame; 64]> per thread, spills to heap
- Epoch management: AtomicU64 global epoch + per-thread flushed_epoch
- Segment files: {db_path}-wal-{thread_id} — one per thread
- Recovery: replay all segment files in epoch order
- fsync policy: unchanged (FULL/NORMAL/OFF still respected per segment)

### Why This Works

- Removes the #1 contention point entirely
- WAL writes are now embarrassingly parallel
- The epoch mechanism provides group commit semantics naturally
- Silo (SOSP 2013) proved this scales to 60+ cores on TPC-C

### Files to Modify
- crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs — remove WAL_APPEND_GATES, add ThreadLocalLogBuffer
- crates/fsqlite-wal/src/lib.rs — add segment file management, epoch-based flush
- crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/lifecycle.rs — commit path routes frames to thread-local buffer

### Testing
1. test_parallel_wal_2t_no_data_loss — 2 threads, verify all committed rows present after recovery
2. test_parallel_wal_8t_no_data_loss — 8 threads, same
3. test_parallel_wal_16t_throughput — 16 threads, measure throughput, assert > 1.5x SQLite
4. test_epoch_advance_flushes_all_buffers — epoch tick, all buffers flushed
5. test_crash_recovery_multiple_segments — simulate crash, recover from N segment files
6. test_checkpoint_merges_segments — checkpoint produces correct single DB file
7. test_fsync_policy_per_segment — FULL/NORMAL/OFF respected per segment
8. test_no_global_lock_in_commit_path — instrument to verify no Mutex acquired during WAL write

### Isomorphism Proof Plan
- Same data written, same recovery semantics, same fsync guarantees
- Only change: write path is parallel instead of serial
- Golden output: same rows visible after crash recovery

### Baseline Comparator
Current: Global Mutex<()> WAL gate (pager.rs:29-43)
Target: Per-thread buffers, zero global locks in WAL write path","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":960,"created_at":"2026-03-20T18:31:35.191386524Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T18:31:35.191386524Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contention","phase1","track-d","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3wop3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3wop3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-20T18:31:35.191386524Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3wop3.2","title":"D2: Shard PageCache into N partitions (PostgreSQL BufMappingPartitionLock pattern)","description":"## The #2 Bottleneck

PageCache is behind a single Mutex<PageCache> (pager.rs:1008). With 8+ threads doing thousands of page reads/writes, all 8000+ operations serialize through one lock.

Current code:
  cache: Arc<Mutex<PageCache>>,
  // Acquired ~35 times across pager.rs for get_page, put_page, evict, dirty, etc.

## Solution: Sharded PageCache (PostgreSQL Pattern)

PostgreSQL solved this decades ago with BufMappingPartitionLock: partition the buffer pool hash table into N shards, each with its own lock.

### Architecture

1. Replace Mutex<PageCache> with ShardedPageCache { shards: [CachePadded<Mutex<PageCacheShard>>; 128] }
2. PageNumber hashing: shard_index = (pgno.get() as usize) % 128
3. Each shard owns its own HashMap<PageNumber, PageEntry> + LRU/S3-FIFO list
4. Operations that need cross-shard coordination (global eviction pressure) use a separate AtomicU64 counter

### Key Design Decisions

- 128 shards: 16x the thread count, so even at 16 threads, contention per shard is ~0.125 threads
- CachePadded: prevents false sharing between adjacent shard locks (64-byte alignment)
- Eviction: local per-shard eviction first; global eviction only under extreme memory pressure
- Pin counting: per-shard pin counter (AtomicU32 per shard, not global)

### Why This Works

- Converts one hot lock into 128 cold locks
- At 8 threads with 128 shards, probability of two threads hitting same shard = 8/128 = 6.25%
- PostgreSQL has shipped this pattern since 2005; proven at 100+ cores

### Files to Modify
- crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs — ShardedPageCache replacing single Mutex<PageCache>
- crates/fsqlite-pager/src/s3_fifo.rs — per-shard eviction policy

### Testing
1. test_sharded_cache_8t_no_deadlock — 8 threads, random page access, no deadlock
2. test_sharded_cache_16t_throughput — 16 threads, measure ops/sec, assert > 2x single-lock
3. test_shard_distribution_uniform — 10K random pages, verify even shard distribution
4. test_cross_shard_eviction — global pressure triggers eviction across shards
5. test_cache_line_padding — static_assert ShardedPageCache shard alignment = 64 bytes
6. test_pin_count_per_shard — concurrent pin/unpin on different shards don't interfere

### Baseline Comparator
Current: Single Mutex<PageCache> (pager.rs:1008)
Target: 128-shard CachePadded<Mutex<PageCacheShard>>","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":720,"created_at":"2026-03-20T18:31:57.480996015Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T18:59:44.575166117Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contention","pager","phase1","track-d"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3wop3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3wop3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-20T18:31:57.480996015Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2495,"issue_id":"bd-3wop3.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"D2 COMPLETED: ShardedPageCache fully integrated into SimplePager/SimpleTransaction/SimplePagerCheckpointWriter. All 13 unit tests pass including 8/16-thread deadlock and throughput tests. Workspace compiles, clippy clean. Key changes: (1) Replaced Arc<Mutex<PageCache>> with Arc<ShardedPageCache>, (2) Removed all .lock() calls since ShardedPageCache uses per-shard internal locks, (3) Fixed patch_page1_header to take &ShardedPageCache.","created_at":"2026-03-20T18:59:44Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3wop3.3","title":"D3: Flat Combining for commit sequencer + SSI validation batching","description":"## Contention Points #3+#5: SSI O(N) handles + CommitSequencer cache-line ping-pong

Two related bottlenecks:
1. CommitSequencer (invariants.rs:93): AtomicU64 fetch_add causes ~50ns cache-line round-trip per commit × 8000 commits = 400us just on atomics
2. SSI validation (begin_concurrent.rs:2085): Each commit locks ALL N active transaction handles — O(N) lock acquisition per commit

## Solution: Flat Combining (Graveyard §14.2)

Under high contention, flat combining is FASTER than lock-free CAS storms. One designated combiner thread executes all pending operations sequentially in hot L1 cache.

### Architecture

1. CommitCombiner struct with a publication list (array of slots, one per thread)
2. When a thread wants to commit: write request to its slot, mark pending, try to become combiner
3. The combiner thread:
   a. Scans all pending slots (in L1 — linear scan of ~16 slots)
   b. Allocates commit_seq for all pending commits in ONE batch (single fetch_add(N))
   c. Performs SSI validation for all pending commits together (ONE scan of active handles)
   d. Marks each slot as done with its result
4. Non-combiner threads spin-wait on their slot (usually <1us)

### Why This Is Brilliant for FrankenSQLite

- Commit sequencer: instead of 8 separate fetch_add(1) with 8 cache-line bounces, ONE fetch_add(8)
- SSI validation: instead of 8 separate O(N) handle scans, ONE scan that validates all 8 commits
- The combiner has all data in L1 cache — sequential execution is faster than parallel contention
- Proven by Hendler et al. (SPAA 2010): flat combining outperforms lock-free at 8+ threads

### Key Design

struct CommitCombiner {
    slots: [CachePadded<CommitSlot>; MAX_THREADS],  // 64 slots, cache-line padded
    combiner_lock: AtomicBool,  // try_lock to become combiner
}

struct CommitSlot {
    state: AtomicU8,  // EMPTY, PENDING, DONE
    request: UnsafeCell<Option<CommitRequest>>,
    result: UnsafeCell<Option<CommitResult>>,
}

### Files to Modify
- crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/invariants.rs — CommitSequencer replaced by CommitCombiner
- crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/begin_concurrent.rs — SSI validation batched in combiner
- crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/lifecycle.rs — commit path publishes to slot instead of direct ops

### Testing
1. test_combiner_single_thread — one thread, combiner executes immediately
2. test_combiner_8t_all_commits_succeed — 8 threads, non-conflicting, all get unique commit_seqs
3. test_combiner_16t_throughput — 16 threads, measure vs current, assert > 2x
4. test_combiner_ssi_conflict_detected — two conflicting txns in same batch, one aborted
5. test_combiner_fairness — no thread starves (measure max wait time)
6. test_combiner_cache_line_padding — slots are 64-byte aligned
7. test_combiner_batch_size_varies — 1, 4, 8, 16 pending slots, all handled correctly

### Baseline Comparator
Current: AtomicU64 fetch_add + O(N) handle locking per commit
Target: Single batched fetch_add(N) + single O(N) scan for all commits combined","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":720,"created_at":"2026-03-20T18:32:23.841324400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T18:32:23.841324400Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contention","mvcc","phase1","track-d"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3wop3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3wop3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-20T18:32:23.841324400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3wop3.3","title":"D3: Flat Combining for commit sequencer + SSI validation batching","description":"## Contention Points #3+#5: SSI O(N) handles + CommitSequencer cache-line ping-pong

Two related bottlenecks:
1. CommitSequencer (invariants.rs:93): AtomicU64 fetch_add causes ~50ns cache-line round-trip per commit × 8000 commits = 400us just on atomics
2. SSI validation (begin_concurrent.rs:2085): Each commit locks ALL N active transaction handles — O(N) lock acquisition per commit

## Solution: Flat Combining (Graveyard §14.2)

Under high contention, flat combining is FASTER than lock-free CAS storms. One designated combiner thread executes all pending operations sequentially in hot L1 cache.

### Architecture

1. CommitCombiner struct with a publication list (array of slots, one per thread)
2. When a thread wants to commit: write request to its slot, mark pending, try to become combiner
3. The combiner thread:
   a. Scans all pending slots (in L1 — linear scan of ~16 slots)
   b. Allocates commit_seq for all pending commits in ONE batch (single fetch_add(N))
   c. Performs SSI validation for all pending commits together (ONE scan of active handles)
   d. Marks each slot as done with its result
4. Non-combiner threads spin-wait on their slot (usually <1us)

### Why This Is Brilliant for FrankenSQLite

- Commit sequencer: instead of 8 separate fetch_add(1) with 8 cache-line bounces, ONE fetch_add(8)
- SSI validation: instead of 8 separate O(N) handle scans, ONE scan that validates all 8 commits
- The combiner has all data in L1 cache — sequential execution is faster than parallel contention
- Proven by Hendler et al. (SPAA 2010): flat combining outperforms lock-free at 8+ threads

### Key Design

struct CommitCombiner {
    slots: [CachePadded<CommitSlot>; MAX_THREADS],  // 64 slots, cache-line padded
    combiner_lock: AtomicBool,  // try_lock to become combiner
}

struct CommitSlot {
    state: AtomicU8,  // EMPTY, PENDING, DONE
    request: UnsafeCell<Option<CommitRequest>>,
    result: UnsafeCell<Option<CommitResult>>,
}

### Files to Modify
- crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/invariants.rs — CommitSequencer replaced by CommitCombiner
- crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/begin_concurrent.rs — SSI validation batched in combiner
- crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/lifecycle.rs — commit path publishes to slot instead of direct ops

### Testing
1. test_combiner_single_thread — one thread, combiner executes immediately
2. test_combiner_8t_all_commits_succeed — 8 threads, non-conflicting, all get unique commit_seqs
3. test_combiner_16t_throughput — 16 threads, measure vs current, assert > 2x
4. test_combiner_ssi_conflict_detected — two conflicting txns in same batch, one aborted
5. test_combiner_fairness — no thread starves (measure max wait time)
6. test_combiner_cache_line_padding — slots are 64-byte aligned
7. test_combiner_batch_size_varies — 1, 4, 8, 16 pending slots, all handled correctly

### Baseline Comparator

All 6 unit tests pass. NEXT STEP: Wire combiner into TxnManager.alloc_commit_seq() for production use. The combiner reduces cache-line bouncing from O(N) fetch_add to O(1) batched fetch_add under high contention.","created_at":"2026-03-20T19:33:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3wop3.4","title":"D4: Replace Condvar notify_all with per-waiter futex notification","description":"## Contention Point #4: Thundering Herd on Condvar

InProcessPageLockTable (core_types.rs:287-293) uses notify_all() on every page lock release. With 8 threads, each release wakes ALL waiters — classic thundering herd.

## Solution: Per-waiter futex or targeted notification

Replace notify_all() with per-page notification using parking_lot::Condvar::notify_one() or a per-page WaiterList.

### Architecture
- Each page in PageLockTable gets an optional WaiterQueue (SmallVec<[thread::Thread; 2]>)
- On lock release: unpark only the thread(s) waiting for THIS specific page
- No thundering herd: only relevant waiter is woken

### Testing
1. test_no_thundering_herd_8t — 8 threads waiting on different pages, release one, verify only 1 wakes
2. test_targeted_wake_correct_thread — waiter for page 47 wakes on page 47 release, not page 48
3. test_no_spurious_wakes — unrelated lock releases don't wake waiters

### Baseline Comparator
Current: notify_all() wakes ALL threads on every lock release
Target: notify_one() or per-page targeted wake","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":360,"created_at":"2026-03-20T18:33:06.717197610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T18:33:22.279350486Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contention","mvcc","phase2","track-d"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3wop3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3wop3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-20T18:33:06.717197610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3wop3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3wop3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-20T18:33:22.278498762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3wop3.5","title":"D5: Epoch-Based Reclamation (EBR) for MVCC version chain GC","description":"## Version Chain GC Contention

Version chain GC currently uses periodic background sweep with arena locks. Under high write load, GC can't keep up and chains grow, triggering backpressure.

## Solution: Epoch-Based Reclamation (Graveyard §14.10)

Replace global GC sweep with EBR: threads pin epoch on entry, retire versions to per-epoch retire lists, batch-free when all threads have advanced past the epoch.

### Architecture
- Global epoch counter (AtomicU64, advanced every ~1ms or N operations)
- Per-thread: current_epoch, retire_list: Vec<RetiredVersion>
- When a version becomes reclaimable: push to retire_list
- When global epoch advances past all threads' pinned epoch: batch-free entire retire_list
- crossbeam-epoch is already a dependency — use it directly

### Why This Works
- No global GC lock — each thread manages its own retire list
- Batch deallocation amortizes free() overhead
- Bounded memory: retire lists grow proportional to thread count × epoch interval

### Testing
1. test_ebr_no_premature_free — pinned thread prevents reclamation of its visible versions
2. test_ebr_batch_free_after_epoch_advance — advance epoch, verify batch free
3. test_ebr_8t_bounded_memory — 8 threads sustained writes, memory stays bounded
4. test_ebr_no_global_lock — instrument to verify no Mutex acquired during GC path

### Baseline Comparator
Current: Periodic GC sweep with arena locks + chain-bound backpressure sleeps
Target: Lock-free per-thread retire lists + batch reclamation","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":480,"created_at":"2026-03-20T18:33:07.542360224Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T18:33:07.542360224Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contention","gc","mvcc","phase1","track-d"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3wop3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3wop3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-20T18:33:07.542360224Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3wop3.6","title":"D6: Seqlocks for hot-path MVCC metadata reads","description":"## Hot-Path Read Contention

MVCC visibility checks and page cache metadata reads currently acquire locks even for reads. At 8+ threads, read-side lock contention becomes significant.

## Solution: Seqlocks (Graveyard §14.9)

Readers never lock — optimistically read data + check sequence counter. On writer conflict, retry. Extremely fast for read-heavy paths with infrequent writes.

### Architecture
- Wrap commit_seq, db_size, visible_commit_seq in SeqLock<MvccMetadata>
- Readers: loop { seq = load(counter); read data; if seq == load(counter) && seq % 2 == 0: return data; }
- Writers: inc counter (odd = writing), write data, inc counter (even = done)

### Testing
1. test_seqlock_reader_no_lock — 8 reader threads, verify no Mutex acquired
2. test_seqlock_writer_blocks_readers — writer in progress, readers retry
3. test_seqlock_throughput_vs_mutex — measure reader throughput, assert > 5x Mutex

### Baseline Comparator
Current: Mutex-protected reads on visibility metadata
Target: Lock-free optimistic reads via seqlock","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":360,"created_at":"2026-03-20T18:33:08.347670512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T18:33:23.016285425Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contention","mvcc","phase2","track-d"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3wop3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3wop3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-20T18:33:08.347670512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3wop3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3wop3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-20T18:33:23.015428361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
